:@.name: active_career_type
:@.description:
Whether this career is an active career type. Active careers appear
in a separate tab in the Join Career dialog, and multi-sim active 
careers will have behavior that allows multiple sims to travel to 
the same career.

Active careers expect career_events to be non-empty at runtime. If
all events are culled (e.g., all events come from an unavailable
pack), then the career is considered non-active. If career events are
started outside the career system leaving career_events empty, then
the career type should indicate that via the DYNAMIC_EVENTS type.

:@.name: allow_active_offlot
:@.description:
If checked, will allow travelling to active career even if sim
is off lot.

A Career Aspiration that we want to activate when the Sim is in
this career.

:@.name: aspirations_to_activate
:@.description:
A list of aspirations we want to activate while the Sim is in this
career. This saves from having to track them when the Sim is not in
the career.

Note: You don't need to tune Aspirations that the Career Level
references directly. But if those aspirations rely on others, then
they need to be tuned here.

:@.name: available_for_club_criteria
:@.description:
If enabled, this career type will be available for selection when
creating Club Criteria. If disabled, it will not be available.

:@.name: block_promotion_tests
:@.description:
A set of tests that if passed will block career promotions. Resolver is a SingleSimResolver 
specific to the sim in the career. Promotions are evaluated EOD, if a sim should be promoted 
but is blocked, then they will be promoted on next EOD after they are no longer blocked.

:@.name: budget_label
:@.description:
The budget label in the panel. Right now, this will
always be the label above the client's budget.

:@.name: help_text
:@.description:
This is the text shown when the player hovers over the question
mark icon in the header.

:@.name: over_budget_label
:@.description: The budget label in the panel if the player goes over budget.

:@.name: payout_label
:@.description:
The payout label in the panel. Right now, this will always be
the label above the payout for the current Decorator Gig.

:@.name: wallet_tooltip_budget_label
:@.description: The budget label in the Simoleon Wallet tooltip.

:@.name: wallet_tooltip_over_budget_label
:@.description:
The budget label in the Simoleon Wallet Tooltip if the player
goes over budget.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: A collection of data used for the Gig Info panel in Build Buy.

:@.name: build_buy_info
:@.description:
If enabled, allows tuning data that will be used in the Gig Info panel
in build buy. Currently, this is only used for the Decorator Gig when
the player is on the client's lot.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description:
The interaction to push a sim to call up their costars. This is added
for gigs.

:@.name: call_costar_interaction
:@.description:
The interaction to push a sim to call up their costars. This is added
for gigs.

:@.name: can_be_fired
:@.description:
Whether or not the Sim can be fired from this career.  For example,
children cannot be fired from school.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The interaction to push on a sim who cancels an audition.

:@.name: cancel_audition_interaction
:@.description: The interaction to push on a sim who cancels an audition.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The interaction to push on a sim to cancel a gig.

:@.name: cancel_gig_interaction
:@.description: The interaction to push on a sim to cancel a gig.

:@.name: career_affordance
:@.description: SI to push to go to work.

:@.name: career_availablity_tests
:@.description:
When a Sim calls to join a Career, this test set determines if a 
particular career can be available to that Sim at all. This test
set determines if a Career is visible in the Career Panel.

:@.name: career_category
:@.description:
Category for career, this will be used for aspirations and other
systems which should trigger for careers categories but not for
others.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description:
The text we want to use to override the title bar in for this career
in the Career Panel.

:@.name: career_display_name_override
:@.description:
If enabled, this will override the Career name in the Career Panel in the 
UI.  This should be used for Careers that allow multiple active gigs, as 
each gig will be appear as its own "career" in the Career Panel.  This will 
use a number token.
Ex: Quest #{0.Number}

:@.name: career_event_decline_option
:@.description:
Select what will happen if the player chooses to decline the career event
confirmation dialog.

:@.name: career_events
:@.description: A list of available career events.
A localized string that may NOT require tokens.

:@.name: company_names
:@.description:
A list of random company names. A Sim's career is assigned one of
these and the selection is preserved.

:@.name: company
:@.description:
Sims go to work in rabbit holes or active career venues. They are assigned a
random company name to define "where" they work.
A localized string that may NOT require tokens.

:@.name: company_names
:@.description:
A list of random company names. A Sim's career is assigned one of
these and the selection is preserved.

:@.name: service_npc
:@.description:
A career location for service NPCs that allows them to work on other sims'
home lots.
The required venue for this career.

:@.name: venues
:@.description: The set of required venue types to be in this career.

:@.name: venue
:@.description:
Sims go to work at a specific venue.

If the lot no longer matches the required venue type, the Sim is fired from
the career.

:@.name: career_location
:@.description: Define where Sims go to work.
A localized string that may use tokens.

:@.name: additional_texts
:@.description: If enabled, add additional text to the dialog

:@.name: anonymous_target_sim
:@.description:
DEPRECATED! Please use custom_mask_target_sim instead.
Use InGame/UI/Icons/Dialog/phone_anonymous_caller_icon.png and UI/Gameplay/Phone:UnknownCallerName.

If this dialog is using a target sim id to give a conversation type view and this is checked, then the
target sim icon will instead be replaced by an anonymous caller.

:@.name: audio
:@.description: The sound to play.

:@.name: immediate_audio
:@.description:
If checked, this audio will be triggered immediately, nothing
will block.

ex. Earbuds audio will be played immediately while 
the Sim is routing or animating.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The name of the slot this audio is attached to.

:@.name: joint_name_hash
:@.description:
Specify if the audio is attached to a slot and, if so, which
slot. Otherwise the audio will be attached to the object's 
origin.

:@.name: play_on_active_sim_only
:@.description:
If enabled, and audio target is Sim, the audio will only be 
played on selected Sim. Otherwise it will be played regardless 
Sim is selected or not.

If audio target is Object, always set this to False. Otherwise
the audio will never be played.

ex. This will be useful for Earbuds where we want to hear the
music only when the Sim is selected.

be used in the case where dialog_bg_style is BG_DYNAMIC_IMAGE.

:@.name: dialog_footer_type
:@.description:
An enumeration of the type for YES BUTTONS and DIALOG BUTTON footers.
AUTOMATIC lets the code decide which footer to use.
YES_BUTTONS_FOOTER is a footer with an OK BUTTON by default.
DIALOG_BUTTONS_FOOTER is a footer with only DIALOG BUTTONS.

:@.name: audio
:@.description: The sound to play.

:@.name: immediate_audio
:@.description:
If checked, this audio will be triggered immediately, nothing
will block.

ex. Earbuds audio will be played immediately while 
the Sim is routing or animating.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The name of the slot this audio is attached to.

:@.name: joint_name_hash
:@.description:
Specify if the audio is attached to a slot and, if so, which
slot. Otherwise the audio will be attached to the object's 
origin.

:@.name: play_on_active_sim_only
:@.description:
If enabled, and audio target is Sim, the audio will only be 
played on selected Sim. Otherwise it will be played regardless 
Sim is selected or not.

If audio target is Object, always set this to False. Otherwise
the audio will never be played.

ex. This will be useful for Earbuds where we want to hear the
music only when the Sim is selected.

:@.name: anonymous_target_sim
:@.description:
DEPRECATED! Please use custom_mask_target_sim instead.
Use InGame/UI/Icons/Dialog/phone_anonymous_caller_icon.png and UI/Gameplay/Phone:UnknownCallerName.

If this dialog is using a target sim id to give a conversation type view and this is checked, then the
target sim icon will instead be replaced by an anonymous caller.

:@.name: audio
:@.description: The sound to play.

:@.name: immediate_audio
:@.description:
If checked, this audio will be triggered immediately, nothing
will block.

ex. Earbuds audio will be played immediately while 
the Sim is routing or animating.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The name of the slot this audio is attached to.

:@.name: joint_name_hash
:@.description:
Specify if the audio is attached to a slot and, if so, which
slot. Otherwise the audio will be attached to the object's 
origin.

:@.name: play_on_active_sim_only
:@.description:
If enabled, and audio target is Sim, the audio will only be 
played on selected Sim. Otherwise it will be played regardless 
Sim is selected or not.

If audio target is Object, always set this to False. Otherwise
the audio will never be played.

ex. This will be useful for Earbuds where we want to hear the
music only when the Sim is selected.

:@.name: audio_sting

:@.description:

dialog is shown.

:@.description: The background image for the family portrait.

:@.name: background_image

be used in the case where dialog_bg_style is BG_DYNAMIC_IMAGE.

:@.description:
An enumeration of the type for YES BUTTONS and DIALOG BUTTON footers.

:@.name: audio
:@.description: The sound to play.

:@.name: immediate_audio
:@.description:
If checked, this audio will be triggered immediately, nothing
will block.

ex. Earbuds audio will be played immediately while 
the Sim is routing or animating.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The name of the slot this audio is attached to.

:@.name: joint_name_hash
:@.description:
Specify if the audio is attached to a slot and, if so, which
slot. Otherwise the audio will be attached to the object's 
origin.

:@.name: play_on_active_sim_only
:@.description:
If enabled, and audio target is Sim, the audio will only be 
played on selected Sim. Otherwise it will be played regardless 
Sim is selected or not.

If audio target is Object, always set this to False. Otherwise
the audio will never be played.

ex. This will be useful for Earbuds where we want to hear the
music only when the Sim is selected.

 0 An object representitive of the group

The token is a single object name. If multiple participants are
returned, an arbitrary one is used. That is, unless the mismatch key is
specified. In that case, the string is used unless all of the
participants' definitions match.

This will use the data passed into the show_dialog()
function. This must be supported with GPE work, so
only use this if you have talked to a GPE.

:@.name: string
:@.description: A string.
An argument being passed to the command.

:@.name: arguments
:@.description:
The arguments for this command. Arguments will be added after the
command.

:@.name: command
:@.description: The command.

:@.name: literal
:@.description: A UI Response that sends a server command.

:@.name: reference
:@.description: A reference to a UIResponseCommand tuning snippet.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: This may be tuned in place here using 'literal' or as a reference to a UIResponseCommand tuning snippet.

:@.name: response_command
:@.description: If enabled, specifies a command to be called by the client.

:@.name: literal
:@.description: A localized string that may use tokens.

:@.name: reference
:@.description: A reference to a LocalizedString tuning snippet.
This may be tuned in place here using 'literal' or as a reference to a LocalizedString tuning snippet.

:@.name: concatenation
:@.description: A list of localized string concatenations. These strings will be joined together into single line-separated string

:@.name: single
:@.description: A localized string that may use tokens.
A localized string that may use tokens.

:@.name: variation
:@.description: A list of possible localized string variations.

:@.name: subtext
:@.description: If tuned, this text will display below the button. Span tags can be used to change the color of the text to green/positive and red/negative. TEXT will make the word TEXT green TEXT will make the word TEXT red

:@.name: text
:@.description: The prompt's text.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The tooltip's text.

:@.name: tooltip_text
:@.description: If enabled, this is the tooltip text to be shown on hover for this button.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: ID of a Tutorial, TutorialCategory, or TutorialSubcategory to be displayed.

:@.name: tutorial_id
:@.description: If set, this tutorial ID will be used for SHOW_LESSONS requests.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: Name of a UI message to be sent.

:@.name: ui_message_name
:@.description: If set, this UI message will be used for SEND_UI_MESSAGE requests.

:@.name: argument_name

:@.name: argument_value
:@.description: The value of this argument.

:@.description:

:@.description: This prompt's associated UI action.

:@.name: ui_responses

:@.description: Specify the notification's urgency.

:@.name: visual_type
:@.description: Specify the notification's visual treatment.

:@.name: career_early_warning_notification
:@.description: Message warning the Sim will need to leave for work soon.

:@.name: career_early_warning_time
:@.description:
How many hours before a the Sim needs to go to work to show
the Career Early Warning Notification. If this is <= 0, the
notification will not be shown.
A localized string that may use tokens.

:@.name: additional_texts
:@.description: If enabled, add additional text to the dialog

:@.name: anonymous_target_sim
:@.description:
DEPRECATED! Please use custom_mask_target_sim instead.
Use InGame/UI/Icons/Dialog/phone_anonymous_caller_icon.png and UI/Gameplay/Phone:UnknownCallerName.

If this dialog is using a target sim id to give a conversation type view and this is checked, then the
target sim icon will instead be replaced by an anonymous caller.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The Cancel button audio event name.

:@.name: audio_cancel
:@.description: If enabled, this is the audio event sent by the Cancel button.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The Ok button audio event name.

:@.name: audio_ok
:@.description: If enabled, this is the audio event sent by the Ok button.

:@.name: audio
:@.description: The sound to play.

:@.name: immediate_audio
:@.description:
If checked, this audio will be triggered immediately, nothing
will block.

ex. Earbuds audio will be played immediately while 
the Sim is routing or animating.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The name of the slot this audio is attached to.

:@.name: joint_name_hash
:@.description:
Specify if the audio is attached to a slot and, if so, which
slot. Otherwise the audio will be attached to the object's 
origin.

:@.name: play_on_active_sim_only
:@.description:
If enabled, and audio target is Sim, the audio will only be 
played on selected Sim. Otherwise it will be played regardless 
Sim is selected or not.

If audio target is Object, always set this to False. Otherwise
the audio will never be played.

ex. This will be useful for Earbuds where we want to hear the
music only when the Sim is selected.

:@.name: audio_sting
:@.description: If enabled, play an audio sting when the dialog is shown.

:@.description:

dialog is shown.

:@.description: The background audio event string.

:@.name: background_audio
:@.description: If enabled, play background audio while the dialog is open.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The background image for the family portrait.

:@.name: background_image
:@.description:
If enabled, add a background image to the dialog.  This will only 
be used in the case where dialog_bg_style is BG_DYNAMIC_IMAGE.

    1. Household Name
    2. Household Description
2. Participants that resolve into zone ids
    1. Lot Name
    2. Lot Description
    3. Name of household that lives there
    4. Description of household that lives there.

:@.name: tokens

A list of tokens that will be returned by this factory. Any string
that uses this token will have token '0' be set to the first
element, '1' to the second element, and so on. Do not let the list
inheritance values confuse you; regardless of what the list element
index is, the first element will always be 0, the second element 1,
and so on.

:@.name: career_event_confirmation_dialog
:@.description:
At the beginning of a work day, if the career has available events and
a single Sim is eligible to do an event (or the career only supports single sim events),
this dialog is shown. If player accepts, the Sim is sent to the career
event (e.g. hospital, police station, etc.). If player declines, the Sim 
goes to rabbit hole to work.

Params passed to Text:
{0.SimFirstName} and the like - Sim in career
{1.String} - Job title
{2.String} - Career name
{3.String} - Company name
A localized string that may use tokens.

:@.description: If enabled, add additional text to the dialog

:@.name: anonymous_target_sim
:@.description:
DEPRECATED! Please use custom_mask_target_sim instead.
Use InGame/UI/Icons/Dialog/phone_anonymous_caller_icon.png and UI/Gameplay/Phone:UnknownCallerName.

If this dialog is using a target sim id to give a conversation type view and this is checked, then the
target sim icon will instead be replaced by an anonymous caller.

:@.name: audio
:@.description: The sound to play.

:@.name: immediate_audio
:@.description:
If checked, this audio will be triggered immediately, nothing
will block.

ex. Earbuds audio will be played immediately while 
the Sim is routing or animating.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The name of the slot this audio is attached to.

:@.name: joint_name_hash
:@.description:
Specify if the audio is attached to a slot and, if so, which
slot. Otherwise the audio will be attached to the object's 
origin.

:@.name: play_on_active_sim_only
:@.description:
If enabled, and audio target is Sim, the audio will only be 
played on selected Sim. Otherwise it will be played regardless 
Sim is selected or not.

If audio target is Object, always set this to False. Otherwise
the audio will never be played.

ex. This will be useful for Earbuds where we want to hear the
music only when the Sim is selected.

:@.name: notification
:@.description:
Notification warning work day is going to end.

 Params passed to Text:
 {0.SimFirstName} and the like - Sim in career
 {1.String} - Job title
 {2.String} - Career name
 {3.String} - Company name

:@.name: time

:@.name: enabled

Tuning for a notification warning the player that their Sim's
active career event is about to end.

more than time tuned. 
If disabled, no notification will be displayed.

Use InGame/UI/Icons/Dialog/phone_anonymous_caller_icon.png and UI/Gameplay/Phone:UnknownCallerName.

If this dialog is using a target sim id to give a conversation type view and this is checked, then the
target sim icon will instead be replaced by an anonymous caller.

:@.name: immediate_audio
:@.description:
If checked, this audio will be triggered immediately, nothing
will block.

ex. Earbuds audio will be played immediately while 
the Sim is routing or animating.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The name of the slot this audio is attached to.

:@.name: joint_name_hash
:@.description:
Specify if the audio is attached to a slot and, if so, which
slot. Otherwise the audio will be attached to the object's 
origin.

:@.name: play_on_active_sim_only
:@.description:
If enabled, and audio target is Sim, the audio will only be 
played on selected Sim. Otherwise it will be played regardless 
Sim is selected or not.

If audio target is Object, always set this to False. Otherwise
the audio will never be played.

ex. This will be useful for Earbuds where we want to hear the
music only when the Sim is selected.

:@.description:

:@.name: row_description_display
:@.description:
How to display the description.

DEFAULT - Show the description in the default way. In some dialogs
(like object pickers) this will ellipsize the description if it's
too long.

NO_DESCRIPTION - Don't show any description.

FULL_DESCRIPTION - Show the full description, regardless of length.
In object pickers, this will cause the description field to grow
with the length of the description.

SINGLE_LINE_DESCRIPTION - Only show the first line of the description
in the picker cell views, up to the first line break. 
Shows the full description in a tooltip.

:@.description:
If checked, this audio will be triggered immediately, nothing
will block.

ex. Earbuds audio will be played immediately while 
the Sim is routing or animating.

:@.name: enabled

:@.name: joint_name_hash

Specify if the audio is attached to a slot and, if so, which

origin.

:@.name: play_on_active_sim_only
:@.description:
If enabled, and audio target is Sim, the audio will only be 
played on selected Sim. Otherwise it will be played regardless 
Sim is selected or not.

If audio target is Object, always set this to False. Otherwise
the audio will never be played.

ex. This will be useful for Earbuds where we want to hear the
music only when the Sim is selected.

:@.name: tokens
:@.description:
A list of tokens that will be returned by this factory. Any string
that uses this token will have token '0' be set to the first
element, '1' to the second element, and so on. Do not let the list
inheritance values confuse you; regardless of what the list element
index is, the first element will always be 0, the second element 1,
and so on.

:@.name: audio
:@.description: The sound to play.

:@.name: immediate_audio
:@.description:
If checked, this audio will be triggered immediately, nothing
will block.

ex. Earbuds audio will be played immediately while 
the Sim is routing or animating.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The name of the slot this audio is attached to.

:@.name: joint_name_hash
:@.description:
Specify if the audio is attached to a slot and, if so, which
slot. Otherwise the audio will be attached to the object's 
origin.

:@.name: play_on_active_sim_only
:@.description:
If enabled, and audio target is Sim, the audio will only be 
played on selected Sim. Otherwise it will be played regardless 
Sim is selected or not.

If audio target is Object, always set this to False. Otherwise
the audio will never be played.

ex. This will be useful for Earbuds where we want to hear the
music only when the Sim is selected.

of notifications in this group that fire exceed the tuned TNS suppression limit.

:@.description: The button audio event name.

id. Make sure this is unique for each response.

:@.description: A boolean value.

:@.name: floating_point

The participant to resolve for this response argument.

:@.name: anonymous_target_sim
:@.description:
DEPRECATED! Please use custom_mask_target_sim instead.
Use InGame/UI/Icons/Dialog/phone_anonymous_caller_icon.png and UI/Gameplay/Phone:UnknownCallerName.

If this dialog is using a target sim id to give a conversation type view and this is checked, then the
target sim icon will instead be replaced by an anonymous caller.

:@.name: audio
:@.description: The sound to play.

:@.name: immediate_audio
:@.description:
If checked, this audio will be triggered immediately, nothing
will block.

ex. Earbuds audio will be played immediately while 
the Sim is routing or animating.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The name of the slot this audio is attached to.

:@.name: joint_name_hash
:@.description:
Specify if the audio is attached to a slot and, if so, which
slot. Otherwise the audio will be attached to the object's 
origin.

:@.name: play_on_active_sim_only
:@.description:
If enabled, and audio target is Sim, the audio will only be 
played on selected Sim. Otherwise it will be played regardless 
Sim is selected or not.

If audio target is Object, always set this to False. Otherwise
the audio will never be played.

ex. This will be useful for Earbuds where we want to hear the
music only when the Sim is selected.

:@.name: audio_sting
:@.description: If enabled, play an audio sting when the dialog is shown.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The participant of the interaction who will play the audio_sting.

:@.name: audio_sting_owner
:@.description:
If enabled, this will set the owner of the audio sting when the
dialog is shown.

:@.name: auto_delete_reason
:@.description: Specify the notification's auto-delete reason.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The background audio event string.

:@.name: background_audio
:@.description: If enabled, play background audio while the dialog is open.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The background image for the family portrait.

:@.name: background_image
:@.description:
If enabled, add a background image to the dialog.  This will only 
be used in the case where dialog_bg_style is BG_DYNAMIC_IMAGE.

the audio will never be played.

music only when the Sim is selected.

:@.name: reference
:@.description: A reference to a UIResponseCommand tuning snippet.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: This may be tuned in place here using 'literal' or as a reference to a UIResponseCommand tuning snippet.

:@.name: response_command
:@.description: If enabled, specifies a command to be called by the client.

:@.name: literal
:@.description: A localized string that may use tokens.

:@.name: reference
:@.description: A reference to a LocalizedString tuning snippet.
This may be tuned in place here using 'literal' or as a reference to a LocalizedString tuning snippet.

:@.name: concatenation
:@.description: A list of localized string concatenations. These strings will be joined together into single line-separated string

:@.name: single
:@.description: A localized string that may use tokens.
A localized string that may use tokens.

:@.name: variation
:@.description: A list of possible localized string variations.

:@.name: subtext
:@.description: If tuned, this text will display below the button. Span tags can be used to change the color of the text to green/positive and red/negative. TEXT will make the word TEXT green TEXT will make the word TEXT red

:@.name: text
:@.description: The prompt's text.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The tooltip's text.

:@.name: tooltip_text
:@.description: If enabled, this is the tooltip text to be shown on hover for this button.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: ID of a Tutorial, TutorialCategory, or TutorialSubcategory to be displayed.

:@.name: tutorial_id
:@.description: If set, this tutorial ID will be used for SHOW_LESSONS requests.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: Name of a UI message to be sent.

:@.name: ui_message_name
:@.description: If set, this UI message will be used for SEND_UI_MESSAGE requests.

:@.name: argument_name
:@.description: The name of this argument.

:@.name: argument_value
:@.description: The value of this argument.
Arguments for the UI message.

:@.name: ui_message_tuned_args
:@.description:
(Requires Ui Message Name to be tuned.)
If set, these message arguments will be used in SEND_UI_MESSAGE requests.

:@.name: ui_request
:@.description: This prompt's associated UI action.
A dialog response.

:@.name: ui_responses
:@.description: A list of buttons that are mapped to UI commands.

:@.name: urgency
:@.description: Specify the notification's urgency.

:@.name: visual_type
:@.description: Specify the notification's visual treatment.

:@.name: lay_off_career_notification
:@.description: Message when a Sim is laid off from a career.
A localized string that may use tokens.

:@.name: additional_texts
:@.description: If enabled, add additional text to the dialog

:@.name: anonymous_target_sim
:@.description:
DEPRECATED! Please use custom_mask_target_sim instead.
Use InGame/UI/Icons/Dialog/phone_anonymous_caller_icon.png and UI/Gameplay/Phone:UnknownCallerName.

If this dialog is using a target sim id to give a conversation type view and this is checked, then the
target sim icon will instead be replaced by an anonymous caller.

:@.name: audio
:@.description: The sound to play.

:@.name: immediate_audio
:@.description:
If checked, this audio will be triggered immediately, nothing
will block.

ex. Earbuds audio will be played immediately while 
the Sim is routing or animating.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The name of the slot this audio is attached to.

:@.name: joint_name_hash
:@.description:
Specify if the audio is attached to a slot and, if so, which
slot. Otherwise the audio will be attached to the object's 
origin.

:@.name: play_on_active_sim_only
:@.description:
If enabled, and audio target is Sim, the audio will only be 
played on selected Sim. Otherwise it will be played regardless 
Sim is selected or not.

If audio target is Object, always set this to False. Otherwise
the audio will never be played.

ex. This will be useful for Earbuds where we want to hear the
music only when the Sim is selected.

:@.name: audio_sting
:@.description: If enabled, play an audio sting when the dialog is shown.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The participant of the interaction who will play the audio_sting.

:@.name: audio_sting_owner
:@.description:
If enabled, this will set the owner of the audio sting when the
dialog is shown.

:@.name: auto_delete_reason
:@.description: Specify the notification's auto-delete reason.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The background audio event string.

:@.name: background_audio
:@.description: If enabled, play background audio while the dialog is open.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The background image for the family portrait.

:@.name: background_image
:@.description:
If enabled, add a background image to the dialog.  This will only 
be used in the case where dialog_bg_style is BG_DYNAMIC_IMAGE.

:@.description: The Participant who owns the lifestyle brand we want to use.

:@.name: participant

:@.name: dialog_bg_style
:@.description: The style background to apply to this dialog.

:@.name: dialog_footer_type
:@.description:
An enumeration of the type for YES BUTTONS and DIALOG BUTTON footers.
AUTOMATIC lets the code decide which footer to use.
YES_BUTTONS_FOOTER is a footer with an OK BUTTON by default.
DIALOG_BUTTONS_FOOTER is a footer with only DIALOG BUTTONS.

:@.name: dialog_options
:@.description: Options to apply to the dialog.

:@.name: dialog_style
:@.description: The style layout to apply to this dialog.

:@.name: expand_behavior
:@.description: Specify the notification's expand behavior.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description:
The participant to be used as the owner of this dialog. If this
participant doesn't exist, the default owner will be used
instead.

:@.name: icon_override_participant
:@.description:
If enabled, allows a different participant to be considered the
owner of this dialog. Typically, this will only affect the Sim
portrait used at the top of the dialog, but there could be other
adverse affects so be sure to talk to your UI partner before tuning
this.

:@.name: information_level
:@.description: Specify the notification's information level.

:@.name: override_string
:@.description: The alternate text that will be displayed in the TNS.

:@.name: override_text_mapping
:@.description: If enabled, we will override the TNS text depending on age

:@.name: participant
:@.description: This field is deprecated. Please use 'icon' instead.

:@.name: phone_ring_type
:@.description:
The phone ring type of this dialog.  If tuned to anything other
than None this dialog will only appear after clicking on the phone.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The button audio event name.

:@.name: audio_event_name
:@.description: If enabled, this is the audio event sent by this button.

:@.name: build_buy_filters
:@.description: If enabled, specified category and pack id will be applied in the build buy mode

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The icon image to be displayed.

:@.name: button_icon
:@.description: If enabled, this is the icon to be shown on this button.

:@.name: dialog_response_id_override
:@.description:
The dialog response id for this response that will override the default response
id. Make sure this is unique for each response.

:@.name: loots
:@.description: A list of loots that will be applied when the player selects this response.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: Loots to apply for the response and dialog response id that will override the original id.

:@.name: loots_for_response

:@.description: A boolean value.

:@.name: floating_point
:@.description: A floating point number.

:@.name: overmax_notification
:@.description:
The notification to display when a Sim reaches an overmax level.
The following tokens are provided:
 * 0: The Sim owner of the career
 * 1: The level name (e.g. Chef)
 * 2: The career name (e.g. Culinary)
 * 3: The company name (e.g. Maids United)
 * 4: The overmax level
 * 5: The new salary
 * 6: The salary increase
 * 7: The rewards, in the form of a string
A localized string that may use tokens.

:@.name: overmax_rewardless_notification
:@.description:
The notification to display when a Sim reaches an overmax level.
The following tokens are provided:
 * 0: The Sim owner of the career
 * 1: The level name (e.g. Chef)
 * 2: The career name (e.g. Culinary)
 * 3: The company name (e.g. Maids United)
 * 4: The overmax level
 * 5: The new salary
 * 6: The salary increase
A localized string that may use tokens.

:@.name: additional_texts
:@.description: If enabled, add additional text to the dialog

:@.name: anonymous_target_sim
:@.description:
DEPRECATED! Please use custom_mask_target_sim instead.
Use InGame/UI/Icons/Dialog/phone_anonymous_caller_icon.png and UI/Gameplay/Phone:UnknownCallerName.

If this dialog is using a target sim id to give a conversation type view and this is checked, then the
target sim icon will instead be replaced by an anonymous caller.

:@.name: audio
:@.description: The sound to play.

:@.name: immediate_audio
:@.description:
If checked, this audio will be triggered immediately, nothing
will block.

ex. Earbuds audio will be played immediately while 
the Sim is routing or animating.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The name of the slot this audio is attached to.

:@.name: joint_name_hash
:@.description:
Specify if the audio is attached to a slot and, if so, which
slot. Otherwise the audio will be attached to the object's 
origin.

:@.name: play_on_active_sim_only
:@.description:
If enabled, and audio target is Sim, the audio will only be 
played on selected Sim. Otherwise it will be played regardless 
Sim is selected or not.

If audio target is Object, always set this to False. Otherwise
the audio will never be played.

ex. This will be useful for Earbuds where we want to hear the
music only when the Sim is selected.

:@.name: audio_sting
:@.description: If enabled, play an audio sting when the dialog is shown.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The participant of the interaction who will play the audio_sting.

:@.name: audio_sting_owner
:@.description:
If enabled, this will set the owner of the audio sting when the
dialog is shown.

:@.name: auto_delete_reason
:@.description: Specify the notification's auto-delete reason.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The background audio event string.

:@.name: background_audio
:@.description: If enabled, play background audio while the dialog is open.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The background image for the family portrait.

:@.name: background_image
:@.description:
If enabled, add a background image to the dialog.  This will only 
be used in the case where dialog_bg_style is BG_DYNAMIC_IMAGE.

:@.name: anonymous_target_sim
:@.description:
DEPRECATED! Please use custom_mask_target_sim instead.
Use InGame/UI/Icons/Dialog/phone_anonymous_caller_icon.png and UI/Gameplay/Phone:UnknownCallerName.

If this dialog is using a target sim id to give a conversation type view and this is checked, then the
target sim icon will instead be replaced by an anonymous caller.

:@.name: audio
:@.description: The sound to play.

:@.name: immediate_audio
:@.description:
If checked, this audio will be triggered immediately, nothing
will block.

ex. Earbuds audio will be played immediately while 
the Sim is routing or animating.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The name of the slot this audio is attached to.

:@.name: joint_name_hash
:@.description:
Specify if the audio is attached to a slot and, if so, which
slot. Otherwise the audio will be attached to the object's 
origin.

:@.name: play_on_active_sim_only
:@.description:
If enabled, and audio target is Sim, the audio will only be 
played on selected Sim. Otherwise it will be played regardless 
Sim is selected or not.

If audio target is Object, always set this to False. Otherwise
the audio will never be played.

ex. This will be useful for Earbuds where we want to hear the
music only when the Sim is selected.

:@.name: audio_sting
:@.description: If enabled, play an audio sting when the dialog is shown.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The participant of the interaction who will play the audio_sting.

:@.name: audio_sting_owner
:@.description:
If enabled, this will set the owner of the audio sting when the
dialog is shown.

:@.name: auto_delete_reason
:@.description: Specify the notification's auto-delete reason.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The background audio event string.

:@.name: background_audio
:@.description: If enabled, play background audio while the dialog is open.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The background image for the family portrait.

:@.name: background_image
:@.description:
If enabled, add a background image to the dialog.  This will only 
be used in the case where dialog_bg_style is BG_DYNAMIC_IMAGE.

:@.description: The Career to override the icon.

:@.description: The Participant who owns the small business we want to get the icon from.

:@.name: participant_type
:@.description: The Participant who owns the lifestyle brand we want to use.

:@.name: participant
:@.description: The Sim who's thumbnail will be used.

:@.name: dialog_bg_style
:@.description: The style background to apply to this dialog.

:@.name: dialog_footer_type
:@.description:
An enumeration of the type for YES BUTTONS and DIALOG BUTTON footers.
AUTOMATIC lets the code decide which footer to use.
YES_BUTTONS_FOOTER is a footer with an OK BUTTON by default.
DIALOG_BUTTONS_FOOTER is a footer with only DIALOG BUTTONS.

:@.name: dialog_options
:@.description: Options to apply to the dialog.

:@.name: dialog_style
:@.description: The style layout to apply to this dialog.

:@.name: expand_behavior
:@.description: Specify the notification's expand behavior.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description:
The participant to be used as the owner of this dialog. If this
participant doesn't exist, the default owner will be used
instead.

:@.name: icon_override_participant
:@.description:
If enabled, allows a different participant to be considered the
owner of this dialog. Typically, this will only affect the Sim
portrait used at the top of the dialog, but there could be other
adverse affects so be sure to talk to your UI partner before tuning
this.

:@.name: information_level
:@.description: Specify the notification's information level.

:@.name: override_string
:@.description: The alternate text that will be displayed in the TNS.

:@.name: override_text_mapping
:@.description: If enabled, we will override the TNS text depending on age

:@.name: participant
:@.description: This field is deprecated. Please use 'icon' instead.

:@.name: phone_ring_type
:@.description:
The phone ring type of this dialog.  If tuned to anything other
than None this dialog will only appear after clicking on the phone.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The button audio event name.

:@.name: audio_event_name
:@.description: If enabled, this is the audio event sent by this button.

:@.description:

Filters include all the buy categories

:@.name: pack_id
:@.description: Pack Id to be filtered in build buy mode

:@.description: Category tag and Pack ID to filter in build buy mode

:@.name: build_buy_filters
:@.description: If enabled, specified category and pack id will be applied in the build buy mode

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The icon image to be displayed.

:@.name: button_icon
:@.description: If enabled, this is the icon to be shown on this button.

:@.description:
The dialog response id for this response that will override the default response
id. Make sure this is unique for each response.

:@.name: loots_for_response

:@.name: days_to_level_loss
:@.description:
When you leave a career, we store off the level you would rejoin the
career at. Every days_to_level_loss days you will lose another level.
i.e. I quit my job at level 8. I get reduced "level 7 right away
because of levels_lost_on_leave. Then with days_to_level_loss set to
3, in 3 days I would goto level 6, in 6 days level 5, etc...

:@.description:
A tunable list of test sets and associated multipliers to apply to 
the moment of a Sim's demotion, to provide the chance that Sim will get demoted. A resultant
modifier value of 0.50 means at the point of work end where performance would require demotion,
the Sim would have a 50% chance of being demoted. Any resultant value over 1 will result in demotion.

:@.name: disable_pto
:@.description:
If checked, will disable PTO references in the UI. This option is
incompatible with setting any pto tuning in the carrer or career 
levels to non-default values.

:@.name: display_career_info
:@.description:
If checked, the full set of career messages are displayed for this
career. This includes notifications when the career is joined as
well as performance evaluation results.

If unchecked, those two sets of data are not made visible to the
player, e.g. for school career.

:@.name: base_value
:@.description: The basic value to return if no modifications are applied.

:@.name: multiplier
:@.description:
The multiplier to apply to base_value if the associated
tests pass.

:@.name: tests
:@.description:
A series of tests that must pass in order for multiplier to
be applied.

:@.name: multipliers
:@.description: A list of multipliers to apply to base_value.

:@.name: early_promotion_chance
:@.description:
A tunable list of test sets and associated multipliers to apply to the percentage chance, 
should the early promotion modifier deem that early promotion is possible,
that a Sim is in fact given a promotion. A resolved value of 0.10 will result in a 10%
chance.

:@.name: base_value
:@.description: The basic value to return if no modifications are applied.

:@.name: multiplier
:@.description:
The multiplier to apply to base_value if the associated
tests pass.

:@.name: tests
:@.description:
A series of tests that must pass in order for multiplier to
be applied.

:@.name: multipliers
:@.description: A list of multipliers to apply to base_value.

:@.name: early_promotion_modifiers
:@.description:
A tunable list of test sets and associated multipliers to apply to 
the moment of promotion. A resulting modifier multiplier of 0.10 means that promotion 
could happen up to 10% earlier. A value less than 0 has no effect.

work.

index is, the first element will always be 0, the second element 1,
and so on.

:@.name: confirmation_dialog
:@.description:
Dialog offered to player to confirm inviting over someone.

Localization Tokens:
0: Sim in career.
1: Sim being invited over.

:@.name: purpose
:@.description:
The purpose for the invite over. This will determine the
behavior of the invited over Sim, as tuned in Venue -> Npc
Summoning Behavior.

:@.name: sim_filter
:@.description: Sim filter specifying who to invite over.

:@.name: invite_over
:@.description:
Tuning that provides a tunable chance for the Sim in this career
inviting over someone at the end of the work/school day. For
example, a child Sim can invite over a classmate after school,
or an adult can invite over a co-worker after their shift ends.

Invite overs will only occur if the player is on the home lot.

:@.name: is_school_career
:@.description:
If checked, the career will test into special behavior and treated
as school for children or teens.

If unchecked, this is a professional career.

Used to branch behavior at the end of the day for re-setting the career
performance statistics for childreen/teens so they may receive
homework help.

:@.name: is_visible_career
:@.description:
If checked, this will be considered a professional career.  If 
unchecked, any career tests that check for a visible, 
professional career will not take this career into account.  In 
addition, any unchecked career will not be affected by any Career 
Loot Ops (because it is invisible).

:@.name: levels_lost_on_leave
:@.description:
When you leave this career for any reason you will immediately lose
this many levels, for rejoining the career. i.e. if you quit your
job at level 8, and then rejoined with this value set to 1, you
would rejoin the career at level 7.

:@.name: preferred_region
:@.description: The Region that should prefer this Career.

:@.name: preferred_region
:@.description:
If enabled, allows tuning a Region that will prefer this Career. 
Currently, that means the Career will be at the top of the Career 
Panel while in this Region and at the bottom when not.

buff tooltip.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description:
The tuning file that applied this buff. This is typically tuned to be the same file that
this buff is being tuned on. This is NOT player facing, and is used for telemetry only.

:@.name: buff_source
:@.description:
The tuning file that applied this buff. This is typically tuned to be the same file that
this buff is being tuned on. This is NOT player facing, and is used for telemetry only.

If this dialog is using a target sim id to give a conversation type view and this is checked, then the
target sim icon will instead be replaced by an anonymous caller.

:@.description:
If checked, this audio will be triggered immediately, nothing
will block.

ex. Earbuds audio will be played immediately while 
the Sim is routing or animating.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The name of the slot this audio is attached to.

:@.name: joint_name_hash
:@.description:
Specify if the audio is attached to a slot and, if so, which
slot. Otherwise the audio will be attached to the object's 
origin.

:@.name: play_on_active_sim_only
:@.description:
If enabled, and audio target is Sim, the audio will only be 
played on selected Sim. Otherwise it will be played regardless 
Sim is selected or not.

If audio target is Object, always set this to False. Otherwise
the audio will never be played.

ex. This will be useful for Earbuds where we want to hear the
music only when the Sim is selected.

owner of this dialog. Typically, this will only affect the Sim
portrait used at the top of the dialog, but there could be other
adverse affects so be sure to talk to your UI partner before tuning
this.

and represent the ok or ok/cancel options in a special way. 
They will use the text as a tooltip for ok or ok/cancel options 
and use particular icons for the buttons.

:@.description: ID of a Tutorial, TutorialCategory, or TutorialSubcategory to be displayed.

:@.name: tutorial_id
:@.description: If set, this tutorial ID will be used for SHOW_LESSONS requests.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: Name of a UI message to be sent.

:@.name: ui_message_name
:@.description: If set, this UI message will be used for SEND_UI_MESSAGE requests.

:@.name: argument_name
:@.description: The name of this argument.

:@.name: argument_value
:@.description: The value of this argument.
Arguments for the UI message.

:@.name: ui_message_tuned_args
:@.description:
(Requires Ui Message Name to be tuned.)
If set, these message arguments will be used in SEND_UI_MESSAGE requests.

:@.name: ui_request
:@.description: This prompt's associated UI action.
A dialog response.

:@.name: ui_responses
:@.description: A list of buttons that are mapped to UI commands.

:@.name: validation_command
:@.description: If set, this command will be called before closing the dialog and send back a response to the UI with the result

:@.name: quit_dialog
:@.description:
This dialog asks the player to confirm that they want to
quit.

:@.name: test_set
:@.description:
If the tests pass, the test-dependent-quit-dialog will
show.

A tuple containing a quit dialog and the test set that must pass to use
specified dialog.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description:
A tunable list of dialog-test pairs. The first test-dependent-quit-dialog to pass will have its 
corresponding dialog used. If none pass, then the default quit-dialog will show.

:@.name: tested_quit_dialog
:@.description:
If enabled and the tuned tests pass, instead of showing the default
quit dialog the tested quit dialog will be displayed.

Example: If a Sim has a scholarship (EP8) that depends on
staying in the career, a tested quit dialog can be tuned to
include a warning in its dialog text.

:@.name: quittable_data
:@.description:
Whether or not Sims can quit this career.
e.g.: Children/Teens cannot quit School.

If the career is quittable, specify tuning directly related to
quitting, e.g. the confirmation dialog.

:@.name: remove_on_death
:@.description: If checked, sims will be automatically removed from this career when they die.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: Statistic used to track professional reputation for this career.

:@.name: reputation_stat
:@.description: If enabled, creates statistic used to track professional reputation for this career.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: Loot to trigger at the beginning of the career

:@.name: scholarship_info_loot
:@.description: If enabled, this loot will run at the beginning of the career si.

:@.name: show_career_in_join_career_picker
:@.display: Show Career In Join Career Picker
:@.description:
If checked, this career will be visible in the join career picker
window. If not checked, it will not be.

:@.name: show_ideal_mood
:@.description:
If checked, displays the current career track's ideal mood in the UI.
Does not change whether the ideal mood affects the career performance.

:@.name: base_value
:@.description: The basic value to return if no modifiers are applied.

:@.name: modifier
:@.description:
The value to apply add to Base Value if the associated
tests pass. Can be negative

:@.name: multiply_by_count
:@.description:
If enabled, will multiply the final test result in tests
by the numeric result of the test if the tests pass. This
only applies for tests that return a count. If you aren't
sure if your test returns a count, ask your GPE and they
can tell you.

Note: This only works on the last test in the test list.
If you aren't sure if your tuning is correct, please try
it out in game to verify that it behaves correctly.

:@.name: tests
:@.description:
A series of tests that must pass in order for the modifier
to be applied.

:@.name: modifiers
:@.description: A list of modifiers to add to Base Value.

:@.name: start_level_modifiers
:@.description:
A tunable list of test sets and associated values to apply to the
starting level of this Sim.

:@.name: start_track
:@.description:
This is the career track that a Sim would start when joining
this career for the first time. Career Tracks contain a series of
levels you need to progress through to finish it. Career tracks can branch at the end
of a track, to multiple different tracks which is tuned within
the career track tuning.

:@.name: affordance
:@.description: The career affordance for this test set.

:@.name: tests
:@.description:
A set of tests that if passed will make this the affordance
that is run to send the Sim to work.

:@.name: tested_affordances
:@.description:
A list of test sets to run to choose the affordance to go work. If 
an affordance is found from this list, it will be pushed onto the 
Sim.

If no affordance is found from this list that pass the tests, 
normal work affordance behavior will take over, running 
'career_affordance' if at home or 'go_home_to_work_affordance' if 
not at home.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: A whim set that is active when this career is active.

:@.name: whim_set
:@.description:
If enabled, this career will offer a whim set to any sim
that is in this career.

:@.name: enabled
:@.description: The description for the work from home.

:@.name: work_from_home_description
:@.description: If enabled, this will provide a description for the work from home
Career Tuning
